response:
Sec. 1  
Allergies to peanuts are immune system responses to proteins found in peanuts  
These proteins trigger symptoms such as itching swelling difficulty breathing  
Living underwater does not eliminate exposure to peanut proteins  
Allergic reactions are not influenced by environmental factors such as water  
People who are allergic to peanuts can still experience allergic reactions if they come into contact with peanut proteins  
This includes accidental ingestion or inhalation of peanut particles  

Sec. 2  
Underwater environments do not provide a safe haven for individuals with peanut allergies  
Allergies are not related to the presence of water  
Medical treatment for allergic reactions requires immediate access to medication such as epinephrine  
Living underwater would complicate the ability to receive timely medical care  
Therefore people who are allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater without significant risk
